articles.title: Rooftop Solar Basics for Homeowners (2024 Update)
articles.summary: A plain-language overview of how rooftop solar works, what it costs, and what homeowners should know in 2024.
articles.content: This 2024 update walks homeowners through how rooftop solar systems work, typical system sizes, incentives, and the steps to go from quote to installation. We cover grid-tied vs. battery systems, net metering basics, and how to evaluate installer proposals.

articles.title: Solar Options for Renters in Apartments
articles.summary: You don’t need a roof of your own to benefit from solar. Learn about community solar, green tariffs, and more.
articles.content: Renters often assume solar is off the table, but there are expanding options: community solar subscriptions, shared rooftop projects, and green power purchasing programs. This guide explains how each option works, what questions to ask landlords or utilities, and how to avoid common pitfalls.

articles.title: Know Your Solar Rights as a Renter
articles.summary: An overview of policies and programs that protect renters who want access to clean energy.
articles.content: This article summarizes key tenant protections, community solar consumer rights, and what to look for in subscription contracts. We highlight state-level policies that expand access and offer template language for asking property managers about solar-friendly upgrades.

articles.title: Should You Add a Battery to Your Home Solar System?
articles.summary: Compare costs, benefits, and use cases for home battery storage paired with solar.
articles.content: Home batteries can provide backup power, maximize self-consumption, and sometimes participate in utility programs. We break down how batteries work, typical sizes and prices, and when they make financial sense depending on your rate structure and resilience needs.

articles.title: Starting a Solar Club at Your Middle or High School
articles.summary: Step-by-step ideas for educators who want to launch a student-led solar club.
articles.content: Solar clubs give students hands-on experience with clean energy. This guide covers forming a core team, finding an advisor, planning simple experiments, engaging with local installers, and using the school building as a teaching tool.

articles.title: Understanding Your Electric Bill Before Going Solar
articles.summary: Learn how to read your electric bill so you can size a solar system accurately.
articles.content: Most electric bills show energy use in kWh, demand charges, and various fees. We walk through common bill layouts, where to find your average monthly usage, and how rate structures affect potential solar savings.

articles.title: Solar Savings Calculator
articles.summary: Interactive tool to estimate upfront costs and monthly savings for a home solar system.
articles.content: Use this interactive calculator to estimate how much you might save with solar based on your location, roof type, and monthly bill. Adjust system size until the estimated monthly savings and upfront costs meet your goals, then save or email the estimate to yourself.

articles.title: Designing Equitable Solar Programs for Your Community
articles.summary: Guidance for local officials on making sure solar benefits reach low-income households.
articles.content: Policymakers can structure incentives, financing, and ownership models to prioritize low-income and historically marginalized communities. We outline best practices, case studies, and metrics for tracking progress.

articles.title: 2024 Electrical Code Updates for Solar Installers
articles.summary: A summary of key 2024 code changes affecting residential and commercial PV installers.
articles.content: This technical brief highlights changes to rapid shutdown rules, wiring methods, labeling, and disconnect requirements relevant for PV installers. Always consult the full code text and your AHJ for final requirements.

articles.title: Intro to Community Solar
articles.summary: What community solar is, who it serves, and how subscriptions work.
articles.content: Community solar lets people who can’t install panels at home buy or subscribe to a piece of a larger shared project. This article explains ownership and subscription models, billing, and typical contract terms.

articles.title: Comparing Solar Financing: Cash, Loans, and Leases (2024)
articles.summary: Pros and cons of different ways to pay for solar, updated for 2024 incentives and rates.
articles.content: We compare cash purchases, secured and unsecured loans, leases, and power purchase agreements (PPAs). Learn how each option affects ownership, tax credits, warranties, and long-term savings.

articles.title: Sharing Solar Savings With Roommates
articles.summary: Tips for renters on splitting community solar or green power costs fairly.
articles.content: If you share a home with roommates, deciding how to split solar savings can be tricky. We offer example agreements, spreadsheet templates, and communication tips to keep things fair and transparent.

articles.title: Downloadable Solar Lesson Plans (Grades 6–8)
articles.summary: Standards-aligned lesson plans and activities for teaching solar energy in middle school.
articles.content: This guide includes ready-to-use lesson plans with learning objectives, materials lists, and assessment ideas. Activities cover energy basics, solar circuits, and designing a simple solar-powered device.

articles.title: First-Time Visitor’s Guide to the Solar Awareness Event
articles.summary: How to make the most of your visit, from workshops to tours and youth activities.
articles.content: Learn how to navigate the event, reserve workshop seats, join solar tours, and find youth education zones. We also explain accessibility services and tips for getting here by transit, bike, or car.

articles.title: Streamlining Solar Permitting in Your City
articles.summary: Practical steps local governments can take to speed up residential solar permitting.
articles.content: We outline online permitting portals, standard plan sets, inspection checklists, and coordination with fire departments. Examples from leading cities show how to reduce soft costs while maintaining safety.

articles.title: Building a Diverse Solar Workforce
articles.summary: Resources for installers and nonprofits to create inclusive training and hiring pathways.
articles.content: This guide highlights community-based training models, apprenticeship programs, and partnerships with schools and workforce boards. We share strategies to recruit and retain underrepresented groups in solar careers.

articles.title: 10 Common Myths About Solar, Debunked
articles.summary: We tackle the most common misconceptions about solar cost, reliability, and performance.
articles.content: From "solar only works in hot climates" to "solar is too expensive," we provide data-backed responses to help you make informed decisions and talk to friends and neighbors about solar.

donation_campaigns.name: Youth Solar Education Fund
donation_campaigns.description: Supports hands-on solar education for K–12 students, including field trips, classroom kits, and youth workshops at the event.

donation_campaigns.name: Solar Access Scholarships
donation_campaigns.description: Provides scholarships and travel stipends so low-income families and students can attend solar tours, camps, and trainings.

donation_campaigns.name: Community Event Operations Fund
donation_campaigns.description: Helps cover venue, accessibility, and staffing costs so the solar awareness event can remain free to attend.

donation_campaigns.name: Solar Learning Infrastructure
donation_campaigns.description: Funds demonstration arrays, hands-on exhibits, and mobile lab equipment used at the event and in schools.

exhibitors.name: Sunrise Solar Installers SF
exhibitors.description: Local residential and small commercial solar installer serving San Francisco and nearby communities.

exhibitors.name: Bay City Solar Co.
exhibitors.description: Full-service solar and battery installer specializing in rowhomes and multifamily buildings.

exhibitors.name: Mission Green Energy
exhibitors.description: Community-focused solar installer offering sliding-scale pricing and energy education in the Mission District.

exhibitors.name: Peninsula Solar & Storage
exhibitors.description: Designs and installs solar plus storage systems across the Peninsula and South Bay.

exhibitors.name: NorCal Solar Pros
exhibitors.description: Regional installer offering residential, commercial, and agricultural solar solutions.

exhibitors.name: Solar Youth Project
exhibitors.description: Nonprofit providing solar education programs and internships for middle and high school students.

exhibitors.name: City Clean Energy Office
exhibitors.description: Local government agency offering information on solar permits, incentives, and energy programs.

exhibitors.name: Sunrise Community Power
exhibitors.description: Local not-for-profit utility showcasing green power and community solar programs.

exhibitors.name: Solar Makers Lab
exhibitors.description: Hands-on maker space featuring DIY solar kits and workshops for families.

exhibitors.name: SunTech Panel Manufacturing
exhibitors.description: Manufacturer of high-efficiency solar panels and inverters for residential and commercial projects.

exhibitors.name: Green Roofs Inc.
exhibitors.description: Designs and installs vegetated roofs that can be combined with solar PV systems.

exhibitors.name: Solar for All Coalition
exhibitors.description: Network of nonprofits working to expand solar access for low-income households and renters.

exhibitors.name: Bright Future Foundation
exhibitors.description: Event sponsor supporting education programs and scholarships for clean energy careers.

exhibitors.name: Solar Garden Apartments
exhibitors.description: Multifamily housing developer showcasing a case study of solar on rental properties.

exhibitors.name: Sunrise Microgrids
exhibitors.description: Developer of community microgrids and resilience hubs powered by solar and storage.

exhibitors.name: City College Solar Training Program
exhibitors.description: Hands-on solar installer training for adults and high school graduates.

volunteer_shifts.title: Thursday Pre-Event Setup Crew
volunteer_shifts.description: Help set up tents, signage, info tables, and the Youth Solar Zone the day before the event opens. Some light lifting required; training and safety briefing provided on-site.

volunteer_shifts.title: Friday Morning Check-In & Greeters
volunteer_shifts.description: Welcome attendees on opening morning, assist with badge pickup, and direct visitors to workshops and tours.

volunteer_shifts.title: Friday Afternoon Info Desk & Wayfinding
volunteer_shifts.description: Support visitors by answering questions, handing out maps, and directing them to exhibit halls, workshops, and tours.

volunteer_shifts.title: Friday Evening Solar Tour Guides
volunteer_shifts.description: Accompany guided solar tours, help keep groups together, and assist the lead educator with Q&A.

volunteer_shifts.title: Saturday Morning Workshop Support
volunteer_shifts.description: Help presenters set up materials, manage seating, and collect feedback forms for beginner and advanced solar workshops.

volunteer_shifts.title: Saturday Youth Solar Zone Volunteers
volunteer_shifts.description: Staff hands-on stations in the Youth Solar Zone, assist families with simple experiments, and reset materials between groups.

volunteer_shifts.title: Saturday Evening Closing & Reset Team
volunteer_shifts.description: Help close down exhibit areas, reset rooms for Sunday field trips, and secure materials overnight.

volunteer_shifts.title: Sunday Early Parking & Transit Crew
volunteer_shifts.description: Assist with directing early-arriving exhibitors and volunteers to parking and bike valet before doors open to the public.

volunteer_shifts.title: Sunday Morning Setup & Registration
volunteer_shifts.description: Set up Sunday program materials, prepare check-in tables, and welcome attendees as doors open. Ideal for first-time volunteers.

volunteer_shifts.title: Sunday Morning Solar Lab Docents
volunteer_shifts.description: Support hands-on demos in the Solar Lab for families and school groups, guiding visitors through simple experiments.

volunteer_shifts.title: Sunday Late-Morning Info Desk (Short Shift)
volunteer_shifts.description: Provide wayfinding help for visitors arriving later in the morning. Shorter shift for those with limited time.

volunteer_shifts.title: Sunday Afternoon Event Cleanup
volunteer_shifts.description: Help with general cleanup, breaking down small exhibits, and sorting materials for reuse or recycling.

volunteer_shifts.title: Sunday Evening Final Breakdown Crew
volunteer_shifts.description: Join the final breakdown team to load equipment, take down signage, and restore the site after the event concludes.

events.title: Opening Keynote: A Solar Future for All
events.subtitle: Kicking off the community solar awareness weekend
events.description: Join local leaders, educators, and youth for an inspiring keynote about how our region is accelerating the transition to solar while centering equity and access.
events.location_description: Large outdoor tent near the main entrance with seating for up to 300 people.

events.title: Hands-On Solar Activities for Classrooms
events.subtitle: Workshop for teachers, after-school staff, and youth leaders
events.description: Experiment with small solar panels, circuits, and classroom-ready demonstrations you can bring back to your students. All materials are provided and aligned to grades 6–10.
events.location_description: Indoor classroom-style space with AV and lab tables.

events.title: Friday Morning School Solar Field Trip
events.subtitle: Guided experience for school groups and youth programs
events.description: A structured field trip for school and youth groups including a guided exhibit tour, rooftop viewing (weather permitting), and a hands-on mini-lab focused on how solar works.
events.location_description: Group check-in desk near the bus drop-off zone; activities occur throughout the exhibit halls.

events.title: Microgrid Facility Bus Tour
events.subtitle: Offsite visit to a local community microgrid
events.description: Ride a charter bus to visit a nearby community microgrid that keeps critical facilities powered during outages. Includes a guided walkthrough and Q&A with engineers.
events.location_description: Buses depart from the tour loading zone behind the Main Hall.

events.title: Introductory Session: Going Solar at Home
events.subtitle: Understanding quotes, incentives, and basic system design
events.description: A step-by-step introductory session for homeowners who are just starting to explore solar. We’ll unpack key terms on installer quotes, explain incentives and tax credits, and show example system designs.
events.location_description: Indoor breakout room near the Education Pavilion entrance.

events.title: Introductory Session: Solar Options for Renters
events.subtitle: Community solar, green power, and talking with your landlord
events.description: Renters can benefit from solar too. This session covers community solar, utility green power programs, and strategies for talking with property managers about solar-friendly upgrades.
events.location_description: Indoor session room across from the Youth Solar Zone.

events.title: Advanced Session: Solar Policy and Rate Design
events.subtitle: Net metering updates, export compensation, and advocacy
events.description: Designed for advanced attendees who want to dig into evolving solar policies. We’ll review recent net metering changes, rate designs, and ways to engage in local and state regulatory processes.
events.location_description: Indoor room adjacent to the Civic Partners Hall.

events.title: Panel Discussion: Local Solar Success Stories
events.subtitle: Cities, schools, and community groups making solar work
events.description: Hear from city staff, school district leaders, and community organizations about successful solar projects and programs in our region.
events.location_description: Outdoor stage near the Community Pavilion.

events.title: Beginner Solar Workshop: Understanding Your Electric Bill
events.subtitle: Saturday evening small-group workshop
events.description: A beginner-level workshop that helps you read your electric bill, estimate your usage, and understand how solar could reduce your costs. Includes a guided walkthrough of sample bills and a Q&A segment.
events.location_description: Smaller outdoor tent with tables for up to 30 participants.

events.title: DIY Solar: Is It Right for You?
events.subtitle: Intermediate workshop on designing and permitting your own system
events.description: For technically inclined attendees considering a more hands-on approach. Learn about design tools, permitting requirements, safety considerations, and when hiring a licensed installer still makes sense.
events.location_description: Indoor lab space with demo racking and inverters.

events.title: Neighborhood Solar Walking Tour
events.subtitle: Visit nearby homes and small businesses with solar
events.description: Guided walking tour of several nearby solar installations. Learn how different roofs, shading conditions, and budgets can still make solar possible.
events.location_description: Meet at the tour flag outside the Main Hall 10 minutes before departure.

events.title: Rooftop Solar Technical Tour
events.subtitle: Behind-the-scenes look at the event’s demonstration array
events.description: Climb to a safe rooftop viewing platform for a close look at racking, wiring, and inverters on the event’s demonstration system. Best for attendees comfortable with more technical discussion.
events.location_description: Safety briefing at the staging area; participants then proceed as a group to the access point.

events.title: Family Solar Discovery Tour
events.subtitle: Kid-friendly tour of hands-on exhibits
events.description: A gentle walking tour for families with children. Visit the Youth Solar Zone, try mini-experiments, and see the main array from a kid-friendly vantage point.
events.location_description: Meet at the check-in table outside the Youth Solar Zone.

events.title: Advanced Session: Batteries and Home Backup
events.subtitle: Designing storage systems and understanding rate impacts
events.description: A deeper dive into battery sizing, backup priorities, interconnection requirements, and how time-of-use rates affect the value of storage. Intended for advanced attendees and energy professionals.
events.location_description: Indoor theater-style room with stage and large screen.

events.title: Community Solar Success Stories
events.subtitle: How shared solar is expanding access
events.description: Learn from program operators and participants in local community solar projects. We’ll cover subscription models, customer protections, and lessons learned.
events.location_description: Outdoor stage near the Community Pavilion.

events.title: Youth Solar Project Showcase
events.subtitle: Short talks and demos from student teams
events.description: Middle and high school students share their solar science fair projects, club activities, and community campaigns in a fast-paced showcase.
events.location_description: Small stage inside the Youth Solar Zone.

events.title: Solar Awareness Fair & Exhibits
events.subtitle: Drop-in exhibits, demos, and activities all day
events.description: Visit exhibitor booths, try hands-on demos, and meet local installers, nonprofits, and city staff. Come and go throughout the day—no registration required.
events.location_description: Indoor exhibit halls featuring installers, manufacturers, nonprofits, and agencies.

event_registrations.event_title: Beginner Solar Workshop: Understanding Your Electric Bill
Advanced Session: Batteries and Home Backup

field_trip_slots.notes: Standard Friday morning field trip aligned with the Friday Morning School Solar Field Trip program.
Late-morning slot for schools arriving after peak traffic.
Afternoon slot currently at capacity; waitlist only.
Pre-event preview for local schools; exhibits in soft-open mode.
Smaller-capacity slot intended for groups with accessibility needs.
Post-event educational day for local schools; focuses on deeper lab activities.
Fully booked by district-wide 7th grade program.
Includes extended time in the Indoor Solar Lab for experiments.
Priority given to schools traveling from 25+ miles away.
Afternoon STEM club-focused slot; includes extra Q&A time with educators.

field_trip_requests.notes: Title I school; requesting scholarship support for transportation if available.
Interested in extra focus on careers in solar if time allows.
Group will arrive early for self-guided time in the plaza if permitted.
Arriving by public transit; flexible to move to a Monday slot if space opens.
STEM academy cohort; will bring lab notebooks for structured reflection.
Requesting Spanish-speaking educator for at least one station if possible.
